SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF WORLD OFFICE PRODUCTS MFG.,INC.,A FLORIDA CORPORAT ION SERVED OCT-20-2003, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT COLLINS, TIM AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO & COMPANY AND TIM COLLINS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT WAS HEARD AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON 12/19/03. RULING: THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 1ST C/A; SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE AS TO THE 3RD C/A; SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH C/A TO PLEAD MISREPRESENTATIONS THAT ARE NOT ABOUT WELLS FARGO'S INTENT TO ENTER INTO THE CONTRACT, IF PLAINITFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH; OVERRULED AS TO THE 5TH C/A; SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO THE 6TH C/A TO PLEAD CONDUCT ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANTS THAT CAUSED INTERFERENCE WITH PLAINTIFF'S CONTRACTS WITH THIRD PARTIES REGARDING THE "OLD MARK", IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH; SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 7TH C/A TO PLEAD CONDUCT ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANTS THAT CAUSED INTERFERENCE WITH PLAINTIFF'S CONTRACTS WITH THIRD PARTIES REGARDING THE "OLD MARK" IF PLAINTIFFS CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER IN WHICH TO FILE ITS AMENDED PLEADING. (301/JLW)

LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUBMITTED ON DEC-19-2003 BY JAMES L. WARREN. THE COURT TOOK DEFENDANT TIM COLLINS AND WELLS FARGO & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, UNDER SUBMISSION. DEFENDANT TIM COLLINS AND WELLS FARGO & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND; 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD MISREPRESENTATIONS THAT ARE NOT ABOUT WELLS FARGO'S INTENT TO ENTER THE CONTRACT, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH; 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED; 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD CONDUCT ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT THAT CAUSED INTERFERENCE WITH PLAINTIFF'S PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE WITH THIRD PARTIES ON THE OLD MARK K AS OPPOSED TO ON THE WELLS FARGO/PLAINTIFF CONTRACT, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gilbert R. Serota, Esq., Howard Rice Nemerovski Canady Falk & Rabkin (415) 434-1600 for Defendant and Moving Party Wells Fargo Bank
Wendi J. Berkowitz, Esq., Morgenstein & Jubelirer LLP (415) 901-8700 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party World Office Products
Neal Sandberg, Esq., Simon, Schindler & Sandberg LLP (415) 901-8700 (Court Call) for Plaintiff and Opposing Party World Office Products
RULING:
Court discloses possible conflict; hearing will proceed. Motion A R G U E D. The Court took DEFENDANT TIM COLLINS and WELLS FARGO & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, 1st CAUSE OF ACTION, UNDER SUBMISSION.
DEFENDANT TIM COLLINS and WELLS FARGO & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND; 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD MISREPRESENTATIONS THAT ARE NOT ABOUT WELLS FARGO'S INTENT TO ENTER THE CONTRACT, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH; 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED; 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD CONDUCT ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT THAT CAUSED INTERFERENCE WITH PLAINTIFF'S PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE WITH THIRD PARTIES ON THE OLD MARK K AS OPPOSED TO ON THE WELLS FARGO/PLAINTIFF CONTRACT, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. (301/JLW/EH)
